requested_goal: cost <= 10 with fully correct train/test/arc-gen/fresh and <=60s verification
status: not achieved; no adoptable improved ONNX was produced
confirmed_rule: For each row, output[:,0:4] is the nonzero overlay of input left columns 0..3 and mirrored right columns 8..5; black is emitted only when both sides are black.

python_rule_checks.provided_train_test_arc_gen: 0 wrong by direct solver in previous audit

cost10_investigation.family: ConvTranspose with input tensor used as W/weights and tiny initializer used as X/seed
positive_findings: ORT accepts scalar/length-1 ConvTranspose bias B with W=input; it subtracts from output channel 0 only, which would solve the black-cell AND bias at cost 1.
NaN seed values create static false masks because 0*NaN propagates NaN in ORT; this can mask columns outside the 4-wide output area without full tensors.
cost10_investigation.blocking_result: After adding channel0 scalar bias and allowing static NaN masking of columns >=4, all seed-width <=9 arithmetic-shift feature sets tested were infeasible. The exhaustive D=1 shift search over lengths 1..9, steps 1..10, shifts -29..3 found no linearly separable solution; best remaining conflict count was 2. Desired-pair coverage with ConvTranspose dilation/stride/pad and seed_width<=9 only yields D=1 candidates, so larger dilation does not give an exact 8-pair reflection within the cost budget.

MaxPool, AveragePool, and LpPool one-node searches over small horizontal kernels/dilations/pads found no exact train+test+arc-gen model. Pooling can OR colors but cannot produce channel0 only when both mirrored cells are black.
It can flip the right side into the left area at cost 1, but it loses left-only pixels and cannot overlay with the original input in a single zero-memory node.

The exact existing model uses a one-node coordinate-polynomial tensor network with 94 initializer elements. Removing the coordinate/channel selector constants breaks the spatial reflection or channel0 rule; replacing them with generated constants would introduce intermediate memory far above cost 10.

verification_summary.scoring_require_correct: not run for an improved model because no correct cost<=10 candidate was found

verification_summary.onnx_structure_check: not applicable to an improved model; baseline checker/shape inference had passed in previous audit

notes: I did not create a replacement full ZIP because substituting any wrong cost<=10 candidate would produce an invalid submission. The correct rule is simple in Python but, under the official cost definition, the one-node static ONNX structures found here cannot express both spatial reflection and black-channel AND at <=10 params.
